ollama-mcp.description: MCP server for interacting with local Ollama models. Provides tools for model management and text generation.

tools.name: check_health
tools.description: Verify Ollama connectivity and health. Checks if the Ollama instance is running and accessible at the configured host.

tools.name: list_models
tools.description: List all available models on the Ollama instance. Returns model names, sizes, and modification timestamps.

tools.name: generate_text
tools.description: Send a prompt to an Ollama model and get a text response. Supports sampling parameters (temperature, top_k, top_p).

parameters.model: Name of the model to use (e.g., 'gemma4:12b')
parameters.top_k: Number of top tokens to consider - optional

tools.name: chat
tools.description: Send a chat message with system and user context to an Ollama model. Maintains system prompts for consistent behavior.

parameters.model: Name of the model to use (e.g., 'gemma4:12b')
parameters.system: System prompt that defines the assistant's behavior

tools.name: pull_model
tools.description: Pull (download) a model from Ollama's registry to the local instance. May take several minutes for large models.

parameters.model: Name of the model to pull (e.g., 'gemma4:12b', 'llama2:7b')

prerequisites: FastMCP and ollama Python packages installed via requirements.txt
setup.environment: OLLAMA_HOST defaults to http://localhost:11434. Override with environment variable if Ollama runs on different host/port.
setup.startup: Ollama server must be running before invoking this MCP server. Start with: ollama serve

playwright-mcp.description: MCP server for browser automation with Playwright. Provides tools for web navigation, interaction, and content extraction.

tools.name: navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Opens the URL and waits for the page to load.

parameters.url: The URL to navigate to (e.g., https://example.com)

tools.name: click
tools.description: Click an element on the page by CSS selector. Waits for the element to be visible before clicking.

tools.name: fill_form
tools.description: Fill form fields (input, textarea) with values. Supports multiple fields at once.

parameters.fields: Object mapping CSS selectors to values (e.g., {'#username': 'john', '#password': 'secret'})

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page and return as base64-encoded PNG. Can capture full page or viewport.

parameters.fullPage: If true, capture the full page; if false, capture viewport only. Default: false - optional

tools.name: get_text
tools.description: Extract text content from element(s) by CSS selector. Returns an array of text strings.

tools.name: evaluate
tools.description: Execute JavaScript code in the context of the page and return the result. Supports full ES6 syntax.

parameters.script: JavaScript code to execute (e.g., 'document.title' or 'fetch(url).then(r => r.json())')

prerequisites: Playwright dependencies installed: `npm install` in mcp_servers/playwright
TypeScript compiled to dist folder: `npm run build` in mcp_servers/playwright
setup.environment: No required environment variables. Playwright uses chromium browser in headless mode.
setup.startup: Requires npm dependencies installed and TypeScript compiled. Run: npm install && npm run build in mcp_servers/playwright
setup.build: npm run build in mcp_servers/playwright to compile TypeScript to dist/index.js

documentation.overview: MCP server configuration for ai-grader project. Provides two MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers: Ollama for LLM interaction and Playwright for browser automation.

installation.ollama:
1. Ensure Ollama is installed from https://ollama.ai/
2. Start Ollama service: ollama serve
3. Pull models as needed: ollama pull gemma4:12b
installation.playwright:
1. Navigate to mcp_servers/playwright
2. Run: npm install
3. Run: npm run build

documentation.usage_ollama: Used in ai-grader to send student submissions and marking schemes to Ollama models for automated grading.
documentation.usage_playwright: Can be used for testing web-based assessment platforms, automated form filling, and screenshot-based submission handling.
documentation.file_format: This configuration uses the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server format compatible with Claude and other MCP-aware clients.
